expectations.evidence: Explicitly called out: 'switching to PostgreSQL is not solving the problem, it's running away'. Provided a decision matrix for when Postgres IS justified vs when it's premature. Quote: '以客户为中心：你需要的是问题被解决，不是技术栈被替换'

Verified each of the 4 claimed fixes: timeout (PRAGMA busy_timeout vs Python timeout are different things), WAL (may not have taken effect), close connections (陷阱1-3 showing common leaks), transactions (DEFERRED vs IMMEDIATE vs EXCLUSIVE). Distinguished SQLITE_BUSY(5) vs SQLITE_LOCKED(6).

Investigated: NFS/network filesystem, multi-process workers, external tools holding locks, file permissions, disk space, Docker volumes, zombie locks. Provided a 5-item root cause ranking by probability.

Provided extensive diagnostic commands: lsof, fuser, df -T, ps aux, sqlite3 PRAGMA, ls -la, /proc/locks, grep for connection patterns. Organized into Phase 1 (30-second triage) and Phase 2 (fix by diagnosis).
